subject: Ecology
title: Water Pollination
description: A quick, practical guide to Water Pollination.

hook.text: A few rare plants use water to deliver pollen—floating it downstream to waiting flowers.

buildup.text: Seagrasses release pollen into ocean currents that carry it to female flowers on nearby plants.

discovery.text: Some freshwater plants release pollen rafts that float on the surface until they bump into stigmas.

twist.text: Water pollination is extremely rare—fewer than 2% of plant species use it.

climax.text: Marine invertebrates may also help pollinate seagrasses, blurring the line between water and animal.

punchline.text: Even the ocean has a pollen delivery system.

quiz.question: How common is water pollination among plants?
options: Fewer than 2% of species use it
